key_insight: Electron beam welding can successfully reconstitute Charpy impact specimens for multiple RPV steels while preserving a dense weld-zone microstructure and near-base-material mechanical performance.
engineering_relevance: This supports a practical route for preparing restructured specimens for reactor pressure vessel safety assessment and nuclear materials testing.
method: Reconstituted 16MND5, 10Cr12Ni3Mo2VN, and X6NiCrTiMoVB 25-15-2 Charpy specimens using electron beam welding.
Characterized weld-zone microstructure with optical microscopy and scanning electron microscopy.
Evaluated Vickers hardness and impact toughness after non-destructive flaw detection.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a compact weld zone with no obvious crack defects, and the 10Cr12Ni3Mo2VN specimens showed the best welding quality with properties closest to the originals.
watch_item: Results are based on a limited set of steels and specimen types, so broader qualification would require additional long-term and irradiation-relevant validation.
paper_title: Effects of electron beam welding on the microstructure and impact properties of three reconstituted steels
